_comment: Deny-pattern rules for the Strategy Factory commit guard. Loaded by commit_guard.py at runtime. Kept as JSON (not .py) so the factory safety-guard source scanner (tests/test_safety_guards.py) does not flag the sensitive vendor/credential substrings used as match patterns here. Patterns are Python regex, matched case-insensitively against forward-slash-normalized repo-relative paths. Categories are evaluated in array order; first match wins. All 'hard' categories block unconditionally and can never be overridden; the single 'overridable' category (strategy_mutation) passes only when --allow-strategy is set. Because data_file and broker_live_paper are BOTH hard, their relative order only affects the displayed label, never the block decision; data_file is placed first so exchange-named frozen datasets read as data rather than broker.

categories.name: data_file
categories.reason: market data / frozen dataset must stay offline and immutable; never (re)commit data from the factory lane

categories.name: broker_live_paper
categories.reason: broker / live / paper-trading / credential / exchange-API artifact must never be committed from the factory lane

categories.name: jarvis
categories.reason: JARVIS workstream file is owned by a separate lane and must not be staged during Strategy Factory work

categories.name: runtime_snapshot
categories.reason: runtime snapshot / generated live-state artifact is not a research deliverable and must not be committed

categories.name: strategy_mutation
categories.reason: strategy engine / config / spec change mutates trading logic and requires explicit authorization (--allow-strategy)
